name: Alabama Home Builders Licensure Board License Verification
updateFrequency: current as of the lookup time, subject to unprocessed information described by the board
licenseTypesIncluded: Alabama residential home builder and roofing licenses issued to individuals and corporations
knownExclusions: Commercial and industrial general contractor licenses are tracked through the Alabama Licensing Board for General Contractors source.
Electrical, plumbing, HVAC, local permits, and municipal registrations are outside this source entry.
The board warns that lookup results may omit information received but not yet processed and are not official verification.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Use the official lookup manually and contact the board when its own no-match guidance calls for clarification.
publicRecordsNotes: The board says search results may be incomplete and directs users to contact it when a record is unclear or absent. A no-match must not be presented as proof that no license exists.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No official bulk download has been confirmed. The public path is a vendor-hosted interactive lookup.
researchNotes: The board regulates residential construction and remodeling. Its official materials also identify roofing licenses and require an unlimited home builder license for qualifying residential solar-panel installations.
maintainerNotes: Keep the board's explicit not-official and potentially incomplete lookup warning attached to every future handoff.

researchEvidence.note: The official board page identifies its vendor-hosted search, states that home builder and roofing licenses are issued to individuals and corporations, and warns that results may be incomplete and are not official.
The board documents residential home builder enforcement and states that qualifying residential solar-panel installation requires an unlimited board license.

blocker.summary: The source is a vendor-hosted interactive lookup whose own board describes results as potentially incomplete and not official; no stable machine-readable interface has been reviewed.
